object_types.name: Student
object_types.definition: A person enrolled at the university, identified by a student number assigned at admission.

source_references.excerpt: Students enroll in courses. Each student is identified by a student number that we assign when they're admitted.

object_types.name: Course
object_types.definition: A catalog entry representing an academic course, identified by a unique course code.

source_references.excerpt: Each course has a course code, like CS101 or HIST230. The code is unique across the catalog.

object_types.name: CourseOffering
object_types.definition: A specific instance of a course offered in a particular semester.

source_references.excerpt: A course offering is a specific instance of that course in a particular semester.

object_types.name: Instructor
object_types.definition: A faculty member who teaches course offerings, identified by employee ID.

source_references.excerpt: An instructor is identified by their employee ID.

object_types.name: Semester
object_types.definition: An academic term during which course offerings take place, identified by a term code.

source_references.excerpt: By term code. It's a short string like "F25" or "SP26."

object_types.name: Title
object_types.definition: The free-text name of a course.

source_references.excerpt: A title and a credit value. The title is free text.

object_types.name: CreditValue
object_types.definition: The number of credits a course is worth, expressed as a whole number.

source_references.excerpt: Credits are always a whole number -- usually 3 or 4, but labs can be 1 or 2.

object_types.name: MaxCapacity
object_types.definition: The maximum number of students that can enroll in a course offering.

source_references.excerpt: Each course offering has a maximum capacity.
Each term has a start date and an end date.
Each term has a start date and an end date.

object_types.name: LetterGrade
object_types.definition: A grade awarded to a student upon completion of a course offering.

source_references.excerpt: The possible values are A, B, C, D, and F.
Students enroll in courses.
Students enroll in offerings, not courses directly.
A course offering is a specific instance of that course in a particular semester.
A course offering is a specific instance of that course in a particular semester.
Each course offering is taught by an instructor.
A title and a credit value.
Credits are always a whole number -- usually 3 or 4, but labs can be 1 or 2.
Each course offering has a maximum capacity.
Each term has a start date and an end date.
Each term has a start date and an end date.
When a student completes an offering, they receive a letter grade.

inferred_constraints.description: Every CourseOffering is taught by some Instructor.

source_references.excerpt: Every course must have an assigned instructor before enrollment opens

ambiguities.description: The transcript initially says 'students enroll in courses' but later clarifies they actually enroll in course offerings. The model reflects the corrected understanding.

source_references.excerpt: Students enroll in courses.
Students enroll in offerings, not courses directly.
